title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
summary: The reviewer wants two things: (1) all new CI helper scripts should compute and `cd` to `REPO_ROOT` so they work from any working directory, not just repo root; and (2) add explicit guard checks with clear error messages—for the admin password extraction in `ci-run-tempest.sh` and for the `flux` CLI availability in `ci-dump-diagnostics.sh`—instead of relying on `set -e` or bare `|| true` to silently handle missing dependencies.
external_feedback:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- The new CI helper scripts (e.g. `ci-build-service-image.sh`, `ci-deploy-operator.sh`) assume they are invoked from the repo root; consider computing and `cd`-ing to `REPO_ROOT` consistently (as you already do in the build scripts) so they behave correctly when run from arbitrary working directories locally.
- In `hack/ci-run-tempest.sh`, extracting the admin password will currently fail with a generic `set -e` error if the secret or key is missing; adding an explicit check with a clearer `::error::` message (similar to the `SERVICE_REF` guard in the image build script) would make local/debug usage much easier.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The new CI helper scripts (e.g. `ci-build-service-image.sh`, `ci-deploy-operator.sh`) assume they are invoked from the repo root; consider computing and `cd`-ing to `REPO_ROOT` consistently (as you already do in the build scripts) so they behave correctly when run from arbitrary working directories locally.
- In `hack/ci-run-tempest.sh`, extracting the admin password will currently fail with a generic `set -e` error if the secret or key is missing; adding an explicit check with a clearer `::error::` message (similar to the `SERVICE_REF` guard in the image build script) would make local/debug usage much easier.

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1
<location path="hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh" line_range="44-45" />
<code_context>
-          kubectl describe daemonset -n chaos-mesh chaos-daemon 2>/dev/null || true
-          echo "=== Events (last 50) ==="
-          kubectl get events --all-namespaces --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -50 || true
-          echo "=== Flux logs ==="
-          flux logs --all-namespaces || true
+        run: hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh
       - name: Upload JUnit report
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**suggestion:** Calling `flux` unconditionally can produce noisy 'command not found' output when Flux isn’t installed.

`|| true` avoids failing when Flux is missing, but still prints `flux: command not found`, which can clutter CI/local logs. Consider guarding this with a check like `if command -v flux >/dev/null 2>&1; then ... fi` so it only runs when the Flux CLI is installed.
</issue_to_address>
~~~

</details>

***

code_comments.body:
**suggestion:** Calling `flux` unconditionally can produce noisy 'command not found' output when Flux isn’t installed.

`|| true` avoids failing when Flux is missing, but still prints `flux: command not found`, which can clutter CI/local logs. Consider guarding this with a check like `if command -v flux >/dev/null 2>&1; then ... fi` so it only runs when the Flux CLI is installed.
code_comments.author: sourcery-ai[bot]
